title: Xueqian (Chelsea) Chen
text:
Xueqian (Chelsea) Chen is a PhD Candidate in Sociology at The Ohio State University. Her research examines how families serve as key sites in the production and reproduction of social inequality across the life course. Drawing on perspectives from family demography and social stratification, she investigates how growing economic insecurity, welfare state restructuring, and rising wealth inequality reshape family relationships, caregiving, and intergenerational inequality.
Using longitudinal survey data, experimental methods, and causal inference techniques, her work explores family dynamics across diverse social contexts, with empirical research spanning China, the United States, and Sub-Saharan Africa.

title: Xueqian (Chelsea) Chen

Family demography ｜ Social stratification ｜ Wealth, debt, & asset
Gender inequality ｜ Life course ｜ Causal inference
